description: Decision trees for Push Chain agent routing and action selection

branches.condition: tx.to is a plain address string (e.g., '0x...')
branches.action: Use Route 1: Execute on Push Chain via UEA

branches.condition: tx.to is an object with { address, chain } where chain is an external chain
branches.action: Use Route 2: Execute on external chain via CEA

details.execution_target: External chain (e.g., Ethereum Sepolia, BNB Chain)

branches.condition: tx.to is a plain address AND tx.from.chain is set to an external chain
branches.action: Use Route 3: Execute on Push Chain with CEA as origin

details.use_case: When execution on Push Chain must reflect an external chain identity

trees.question: What signer library is the user or developer using?

branches.condition: User has an ethers.js Wallet or Signer (v5 or v6)
branches.action: Use PushChain.utils.signer.toUniversal(ethersSigner)

details.provider_note: RPC URL determines chain: Sepolia RPC → Ethereum Sepolia account

branches.condition: User has a viem WalletClient
branches.action: Use PushChain.utils.signer.toUniversal(viemWalletClient)

branches.condition: User has a Solana Keypair (@solana/web3.js)
branches.action: Use PushChain.utils.signer.toUniversalFromKeypair(keypair, { chain, library })

branches.condition: User needs a custom signer implementation (no supported library)
branches.action: Construct a UniversalSignerSkeleton and pass to toUniversal

trees.question: What type of application is being built?

branches.condition: Building a React frontend with wallet connection UI
branches.action: Use @pushchain/ui-kit with PushUniversalWalletProvider

details.benefit: Abstracts wallet connection, signer creation, and client initialization

branches.condition: Building a backend service, bot, or script
branches.action: Use @pushchain/core directly

branches.condition: Building a React frontend but need full control over wallet logic
branches.action: Use @pushchain/core with custom wallet integration

details.note: You manage wallet connection yourself, then create UniversalSigner and initialize client

branches.condition: User rejected signature (progress event SEND-TX-04-04)
branches.action: Display user-friendly message and allow retry

branches.condition: Gas estimation failed
branches.action: Check account balance and contract validity

checks: Check if user has sufficient funds

branches.condition: Transaction reverted on-chain (progress event SEND-TX-99-02)
branches.action: Inspect revert reason and fix contract call

details.debug: Check progressHook message for revert reason

branches.condition: UEA requires upgrade (getAccountStatus shows requiresUpgrade: true)
branches.action: Call pushChainClient.upgradeAccount() before retrying

details.note: Gasless operation, handled automatically by SDK

branches.condition: Network or RPC error
branches.action: Retry with exponential backoff or use custom RPC

trees.question: How should transaction confirmation be tracked?

branches.condition: Need simple confirmation wait after sendTransaction
branches.action: Use tx.wait() on the returned transaction response

details.returns: Transaction receipt with status, logs, gas used
details.best_for: Simple sequential flows where you wait for confirmation before proceeding

branches.condition: Need to track a transaction by hash (e.g., resuming tracking, monitoring)
branches.action: Use pushChainClient.universal.trackTransaction(txHash)

details.best_for: Tracking transactions you didn't send, resuming after page reload

branches.condition: Building UI with real-time progress updates
branches.action: Use progressHook callback in sendTransaction

details.events: SEND-TX-01 through SEND-TX-99-xx events provide granular status

trees.question: How will gas fees be paid?

branches.condition: User has native tokens on their origin chain (ETH on Ethereum, SOL on Solana)
branches.action: SDK handles fee abstraction automatically

details.how_it_works: User pays in native token on origin chain; SDK converts and funds UEA
details.no_pc_required: Users do not need to hold PC tokens for gas

branches.condition: User is on Push Chain natively
branches.action: User pays gas directly in PC (smallest unit: uPC)

branches.condition: User wants to move funds as part of transaction
branches.action: Use tx.funds parameter to atomically move assets

details.behavior: Asset movement and execution happen atomically
